01
Welcome

The ultimate goal at Juliette Calaf Interiors is for you to enjoy a collaborative and stress-free design experience that results in a beautiful finished project. Our rigorous design sensibility and comprehensive process allows us to create a space that tells your unique story and serves as the backdrop for the life you live in it.

We specialize in creating spaces with presence, grounded in proportion, architectural balance, and a deep respect for craftsmanship. The result is a quiet, confident aesthetic that feels collected and uniquely yours.

Our work has been featured in the Wall Street Journal, Traditional Home, Business of Home, Modo de Vida, and several other publications.

"Our role is to understand how you use the space and translate that into an environment that feels natural, refined, and built to endure."

Juliette Calaf, Creative Director
Style Your Story
juliette@juliettecalaf.com
juliette CALAF interiors
Deeter
Oceanica
02
About This Project

The Deeter residence at Oceanica, 2 Calle Nairn, Condado, San Juan is a 3,829 square foot condominium with exceptional bones. The vision for this home is rooted in warmth and intention: a Warm Modern Organic direction that prioritizes natural materials throughout, with a palette built on texture contrast rather than color. Woven through that foundation is a thread of Old World European sensibility, the kind of collected, time-worn quality that makes a space feel like it was curated over decades rather than assembled all at once.

The wellness room is an important part of this project. Our client is deeply health-oriented, and the sauna, cold plunge, and recovery space reflect that commitment. Every material proposed for high-humidity or high-contact areas will be documented for porosity and antimicrobial properties before it is approved, and every specification in these spaces will be made with longevity and performance in mind.

We understand this is a competitive process. What we bring to it is a design sensibility earned room by room, a procurement network that protects your investment, and a process designed to make this feel easy rather than exhausting.

Deeter
Oceanica
03
Scope of Work
The images below represent our initial design direction for each area of the home. We will refine and develop these concepts collaboratively with you once we begin.
Concept Direction — Foyer
Entry / Foyer

The foyer will establish the tone for everything that follows: tactile wall finishes, considered lighting, and a curated furniture edit that creates a genuine moment of arrival before the space opens up.

Concept Direction — Living Room
Living Room

Curved, sculptural seating will soften the architecture, layered with natural textiles and a statement rug. The living room is where the Warm Modern Organic direction will be most fully expressed.

Concept Direction — Dining Room
Dining Room

A statement pendant anchors the room, with a table and seating selection suited to both everyday meals and more formal gatherings. Rug and window treatment define the zone within the open plan.

Style Your Story
juliette@juliettecalaf.com
juliette CALAF interiors
Deeter
Oceanica
Concept Direction — Kitchen
Kitchen

Statement stone, rich wood tones, and cabinetry that reads as bespoke. Island, countertops, backsplash, cabinetry finish direction, and bar seating specified as a fully cohesive composition. Construction coordination included in the full scope.

Concept Direction — Bedrooms
Bedrooms

The master suite is designed as the home's most private sanctuary. Guest bedrooms carry the same material language with their own identity. Custom millwork design for the master suite, walk-in closet, and office is included in the full scope.

Concept Direction — Bathrooms & Wellness
Bathrooms & Wellness

The wellness room is the heart of this project. Every material will be documented for porosity and antimicrobial properties before approval. All bathrooms fully specified including tile, vanity, fixtures, and decorative lighting, with construction coordination in the full scope.

Option One — Limited Scope
04A
Limited Scope Design Fee

Furnishing and styling only, all rooms at $18/SF. This scope covers two to three conceptual floor plan options and a two-style material lookbook presentation. This work forms the foundation of any full engagement and is credited in full toward the full scope fee if you choose to proceed.

Option Two — Full Scope
04B
Full Scope Design Fee

Complete interior design services across all four phases. Rooms involving construction are billed at $36/SF. Rooms with custom millwork design are also billed at $36/SF. Furnishing and styling only rooms are billed at $18/SF.

07
Our Process
01
Conceptual Development
Study of existing conditions, space planning, mood boards, floor plans, and design concepts. Construction timeline and aesthetic direction established.
02
Design Development
Specification of all finishes, fixtures, furnishings, window coverings, decorative lighting, and custom millwork direction. First design presentation held.
03
Procurement
Purchasing proposals issued for client approval. No item ordered without signed proposal and required payment. Vendor coordination and logistics managed by JCI.
04
Installation
Coordination of delivery, installation, and final styling. Two installation days included in the flat fee. Additional site visits billed hourly under project management.

Full-scope projects with custom fabrication typically run 18 to 24 months. A detailed project schedule is provided at kickoff.*

* Timeline depends on scope, client approvals, vendor lead times, and construction schedule.

08
What is Included
  • Full creative direction across all four service phases
  • Space planning, mood boards, floor plans, and ceiling plans
  • Finish, fixture, and FF&E selection and specification
  • Custom millwork design and contractor coordination
  • Anti-fungal and porosity documentation for all wet-area materials
  • Two rounds of revisions per deliverable
  • Procurement management and vendor coordination
  • FF&E logistics and storage coordination
  • Two installation days included in the flat fee
  • Final styling and accessory curation
Not included:
Permit applications, structural or engineering services, renderings (available as an additional service), revisions beyond two rounds per phase.
